title: Who holds US Treasury securities overseas?
gist: Using newly added Treasury International Capital (TIC) data, the post ranks the nine largest foreign holders of US Treasuries as of March 2026 — led by Japan, the UK, and Mainland China — noting these nine hold ~45% of foreign-held Treasuries, a share stable since the early 2000s though individual rankings have shifted.

insights.claim: As of March 2026, the nine largest foreign holders of US Treasury securities were, in descending order, Japan, the United Kingdom, Mainland China, France, Canada, Belgium, Ireland, the Cayman Islands, and Luxembourg.
insights.speaker: St. Louis Fed — FRED Blog
insights.so_what: Japan and the UK now top China as the largest official/foreign Treasury holders — watch this ranking for shifts in foreign demand at auctions.
insights.locator: In March 2026, the nine largest foreign holders

insights.claim: These nine countries collectively hold about 45 percent of foreign-held US Treasuries, a share that has remained relatively stable since the early 2000s.
insights.speaker: Andrew McCallum et al.
insights.so_what: Foreign Treasury holdings are concentrated yet stable in aggregate, so concentration risk is more about which countries hold than the total foreign share.
insights.quote: these nine countries collectively hold about 45 percent of foreign-held U.S. Treasuries, a share that has remained relatively stable since the early 2000s.
insights.locator: This May 2026 FEDS Note by Andrew McCallum et al.

insights.claim: The majority of US Treasury securities are held by the American public and institutions, including the Federal Reserve, rather than by foreign holders.
insights.speaker: St. Louis Fed — FRED Blog
insights.so_what: Domestic demand dominates the Treasury market, tempering fears that foreign selling alone could dictate yields.
insights.locator: The majority of Treasury securities are held by the American public

insights.claim: FRED recently added Treasury International Capital (TIC) system data, giving a global view of cross-border securities positions.
insights.speaker: St. Louis Fed — FRED Blog
insights.so_what: New TIC series in FRED let analysts track foreign Treasury demand by country directly.
insights.locator: FRED recently added data from the Treasury International Capital
